thread.title: Keir Starmer warned that elderly people 'are going to die' as fears mount over winter fuel cuts
thread.title_full: Keir Starmer warned that elderly people 'are going to die' as fears mount over winter fuel cuts

author: Archie Mitchell
title: Keir Starmer warned that elderly people 'are going to die' as fears mount over winter fuel cuts
text:
Keir Starmer was confronted during a round of local radio interviews by a listener named Maxine who said 'we just can't keep warm' and that 'elderly people are literally going to die' Sign up for the View from Westminster email for expert analysis straight to your inbox Get our free View from Westminster email Get our free View from Westminster email SIGN UP I would like to be emailed about offers, events and updates from The Independent. Read our privacy policy
Sir Keir Starmer has been warned "people are going to die" as a result of his decision to scrap winter fuel payments for millions of pensioners.
The prime minister was confronted during a round of local radio interviews by a listener named Maxine who said "we just can't keep warm" and that "elderly people are literally going to die".
"How is he going to live with that?" Maxine asked. She added: "Is he going to have the courage to back down from a terrible decision?"
open image in gallery Keir Starmer was confronted over the winter fuel payment cut ( PA Wire )
Speaking to BBC Radio Lincolnshire, Sir Keir said: "At the moment, until we bring in the changes, the allowance is paid to everyone, irrespective of whether they need it or not.
"A lot of pensioners will say 'I don't actually need it', and I had to answer what difficult decisions we can make to ensure we use our money most effectively."
The PM went on to talk about boosting funding for the NHS to bring down waiting lists, and encouraged those eligible for pension credits to ensure they take them up.
The row comes days after the government's official analysis showed that the decision to means test winter fuel payments, announced in July by Rachel…
Archie Mitchell
